name: John Cornelius
title: Training, Standardization & Service Enablement Leader
summary: Operations and training leader with a manufacturing improvement base and a 20-year Army aviation standardization background. Builds SOP-linked training, evaluation routines, readiness systems, and practical analytics that help teams work safely, consistently, and with less rework. Best positioned for roles where service quality, technical enablement, field readiness, and process discipline matter more than generic customer-service metrics.

Operations Enablement: Safety, quality, production, and logistics coordination
Change adoption with operators and supervisors
Analytics & Tools: Power BI, Python, SQL, MES, ERP, and SCADA data

experience.title: AI & Six Sigma Lead / Operations & Quality Leader
duties: Developed courses, SOP-linked micro-modules, practical exercises, and competency evaluations for production, safety, quality, and logistics training.
Built Level 1-3 evaluation instruments and used training data to revise content and improve time-to-qualification.
Train and coach 60+ Green Belts and analysts on structured problem solving, data analysis, practical AI tools, and business-case communication.
Build Power BI and Python analytics workflows for loss analysis, SPC, project tracking, and operational decision support.
Translate technical and analytical work into clear business language for plant managers, functional leaders, and senior stakeholders.
achievements: Eliminated 15-minute pre-shift handovers by building an automated Power Apps shift brief system, saving $16.8K per year.
Cut JHA review time by 75% using a ChatGPT API workflow with company-specific vector retrieval.
Manage a 40+ project DMAIC and continuous-improvement portfolio tied to $4.2M+ in annualized savings.
Lead and support 30+ Rapid Improvement Events annually, helping teams define problems, test countermeasures, document results, and present business impact.

experience.title: Army Aviation Standardization & Training Leader (Retired CW3)
duties: Managed aircrew training and Electronic Flight Bag programs, maintaining current DoD flight information and digital procedures.
Built training, evaluation, standards, and risk-management systems that improved readiness, accountability, and disciplined execution.
Led standardization and operational excellence across multiple organizations through structured evaluation and training programs.
Used post-mission debriefs, root-cause analysis, and standards reviews to improve training throughput, cycle adherence, and operational learning.
achievements: Designed and executed learning and development programs that progressed 55 pilots to mission-qualified status.
Mentored 25 pilots to pilot-in-command status while conducting standardization evaluations.
Led revision of USAACE Regulation 350-3, consolidating two regulations and streamlining aircraft scheduling.
Completed a 20-year Army career with UH-60/MH-60 aviation, instructor pilot, and operations leadership experience.

education.degree: M.S. - Unmanned Systems, Human Factors Engineering

training: U.S. Army aviation standardization, instructor-pilot, and readiness training
affiliations: Civil Air Patrol - sUAS training and community programs
U.S. Army veteran; community volunteer leadership in Eastern Panhandle, WV
